label: Toxicity Filter
description: ADMET-AI hard-gate toxicity filter — rejects compounds that exceed route-specific toxicity thresholds (hERG, AMES, DILI, ClinTox, etc.).

defaultInputs.name: Input Compounds
defaultInputs.tooltip: Table of compounds including at least a SMILES column and basic properties.

defaultOutputs.name: Passed Compounds
defaultOutputs.tooltip: Compounds that passed all hard-gate toxicity filters (name + smiles), pipe-able to downstream modules.

defaultOutputs.name: Full Results
defaultOutputs.tooltip: Per-compound detail: status, scores, reject reasons, and alerts.

defaultOutputs.name: Summary
defaultOutputs.tooltip: Aggregate counts: total, passed, rejected, route, mode.

configSchema.label: Compound List
configSchema.tooltip: Compounds for ADMET prediction. Provide SMILES strings or connect from an upstream node.

configSchema.label: ADMET Prediction Model
configSchema.tooltip: ADMET engine used for toxicity scoring. Only ADMET-AI is currently available.

configSchema.label: Administration Route
configSchema.tooltip: Administration route determines which hard-gate thresholds are applied (e.g., DILI for oral, Skin_Reaction for dermal).

configSchema.label: Threshold Mode
configSchema.tooltip: Basic uses built-in route thresholds. Advanced lets you override individual hard-gate values.

configSchema.label: Evaluate Drug-Likeness
configSchema.tooltip: Run Lipinski / Veber / Egan / Muegge rule checks (oral route only). Requires the RDKit descriptor service.

configSchema.label: Custom Thresholds
configSchema.tooltip: Override individual ADMET-AI hard-gate thresholds. Each entry maps a field name to an [operator, value] pair.
